stories.body: Former health minister Zubir Ahmed told BBC Radio 4 that cabinet colleagues have pressed Prime Minister Keir Starmer to accept the “unsustainable” situation and set a timetable for stepping down, with allied reporting from France 24 noting Observer claims that Starmer could announce plans as soon as Monday despite his public insistence on staying in office.
stories.headline: Top UK officials expect Keir Starmer to outline resignation timetable

stories.body: With 99.99% of ballots counted, far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella secured 49.66% of votes—just 250,830 more than leftist rival Iván Cepeda—marking a regional swing to the right backed by Donald Trump as Cepeda demands a final count and alleges irregularities, according to The Guardian and France 24.
stories.headline: Trump-backed outsider Abelardo de la Espriella wins razor-thin Colombian presidential election

stories.body: Mediators Qatar and Pakistan said US and Iranian negotiators agreed to a 60-day roadmap that includes communications to keep the Strait of Hormuz open and a Lebanon de-confliction cell, even as Donald Trump threatened renewed strikes and Iran briefly reinstated a Hormuz blockade amid Hezbollah-Israel clashes, per The Guardian and France 24.
stories.headline: US and Iran report ‘encouraging progress’ in Switzerland talks despite Hormuz shutdown

stories.body: Iran’s foreign minister Abbas Araqchi told Lebanon’s Al Mayadeen that Tehran will only seal a broader accord with Washington if Israel withdraws from southern Lebanon and a Hezbollah-Israel ceasefire is secured, reiterating support for the militia even as Hormuz shipping remains constrained and regional strikes continue, Reuters reported.
stories.headline: Iran doubles down on support for Hezbollah, insisting on Israeli withdrawal before any wider peace deal

stories.body: After air losses on Russia’s Zvezda module doubled to two pounds per day, NASA briefly ordered five astronauts into their Crew Dragon safe haven, but rescinded the evacuation two hours later once Roscosmos paused controversial repair work, with both agencies pledging a joint plan to seal remaining leaks on the 27-year-old orbital complex, Reuters said.
stories.headline: NASA reverses ISS evacuation order after Russian air leak deemed under control

stories.body: Temperatures near 40–42C led French authorities to issue red heat alerts for roughly half of mainland departments, close schools, restrict alcohol at Fête de la Musique events, bolster water access and policing in Paris, and warn the heatwave could break national records, according to BBC News and The Guardian, with neighboring Italy and Spain also on high alert.
stories.headline: France places half the country on red alert as dangerous heatwave cancels events and bans alcohol

stories.body: Operation Minjiang officers found 2.7 tonnes of cocaine worth about A$816 million stashed in plastic tubs inside underground bunkers beneath false floors in shipping containers on a Londonderry property, arresting two men who allegedly fled the raid and linking the haul to a syndicate that landed the drugs via Midge Point in Queensland, per BBC News and The Guardian.
stories.headline: Australian police seize record three-tonne cocaine cache hidden beneath Sydney home

stories.body: In a 52-47 party-line vote, Senate Republicans advanced $70 billion in new funding for Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Border Patrol while defeating amendments to permanently eliminate Donald Trump’s $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund, leaving House consideration for next week and underscoring GOP divisions ahead of the November midterms, Reuters reported.
stories.headline: US Senate approves $70 billion immigration enforcement bill while sparing Trump’s ‘anti-weaponization’ fund

stories.body: A massive warehouse fire near downtown Los Angeles darkened skies and triggered hazardous-air shelter-in-place orders on Wednesday, prompting Governor Gavin Newsom to declare an emergency as officials warned residents about toxic smoke while firefighters battled the blaze in the Boyle Heights area, the Associated Press reported.
stories.headline: California declares emergency over Los Angeles warehouse inferno blanketing region in smoke

stories.body: AP obtained internal records and whistleblower testimony indicating that between 2023 and 2025 the DEA repeatedly let shipments of fentanyl pills flow through Albuquerque to build cases against larger networks, a tactic agents and Justice Department guidelines warn endangers the public, with overdose deaths in New Mexico rising 21% even as nationwide fatalities fell.
stories.headline: Records show DEA let staggering quantities of fentanyl reach streets while monitoring traffickers

stories.body: Judge Juan Carlos Peinado has opened a criminal trial against prime minister Pedro Sánchez’s wife, Begoña Gómez, on charges including influence peddling and corruption, ordered her passport confiscated, barred her from leaving Spain, and required twice-monthly court check-ins alongside aide Cristina Álvarez after accepting petitions from the Hazte Oír-led private prosecution, El País reported.
stories.headline: Judge Peinado orders Begoña Gómez to stand trial, seizes her passport and imposes travel ban

stories.body: Spain’s General Council of the Judiciary will meet Monday to decide whether to discipline Judge Peinado for stating in his ruling that Gómez’s police escorts could aid her escape, remarks that Interior Minister Fernando Grande-Marlaska and police unions condemned as an affront to the security forces and that could constitute a serious breach of respect under judicial law, according to El Mundo and RTVE.
stories.headline: Judicial council to review Judge Peinado after he suggested police escorts might help Begoña Gómez flee

stories.body: El País reports that despite fierce criticism of the amnesty law, the conservative Popular Party is now pragmatically engaging with Carles Puigdemont’s Junts to advance its interests, validating Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez’s claim that reconciliation policies forced national conservatives to once again treat Catalan and Basque right-wing forces as legitimate negotiating partners.
stories.headline: Spain’s Popular Party shifts from confrontation to tactical cooperation with Puigdemont’s Junts

stories.body: Veteran councillor Jordi Martí secured 253 of 628 ballots (40.3%) in Junts per Catalunya’s Barcelona primary, defeating Pilar Calvo, Jaume Alonso-Cuevillas and Glòria Freixa to become the party’s 2027 mayoral candidate after campaigning on his close ties to former mayor Xavier Trias and overcoming skepticism from the party leadership, betevé reported.
stories.headline: Jordi Martí wins Junts primary to lead Barcelona mayoral bid in 2027

stories.body: Barcelona will debut 11 new surveillance cameras along the Barceloneta seafront during the Sant Joan festivities, enforce a 6 a.m. beach evacuation for cleaning crews supported by 532 workers and 223 vehicles, limit chiringuito hours to 3:30 a.m., run metro service all night with extra trains, and deploy “purple points” plus safe routes to deter sexual violence, El Periódico reported.
stories.headline: Barcelona to activate new beach surveillance cameras and dawn clear-outs for Sant Joan revelers

stories.body: Barcelona has begun a €2.3 million restoration of the 1883 Umbracle in Parc de la Ciutadella, aiming within a year to repair façades, structures, roofing, drainage, accessibility and fire protection so the botanical pavilion can reopen as a public science outreach space within the city’s Ciutadella del Coneixement innovation plan, El Periódico reported.
stories.headline: Restoration begins on Ciutadella Park’s historic Umbracle greenhouse

stories.body: Mossos d’Esquadra officers, already conducting a major anti-theft operation at Sónar, detained a man near Fira Gran Via after locating discarded phone cases and recovering 34 stolen devices—some already returned to owners—adding to a weekend tally that included multiple drug-trafficking arrests at the electronic music festival, betevé reported.
stories.headline: Police arrest suspect hiding 34 stolen phones at Barcelona’s Sónar festival
